title: Design and implement agent-ergonomic CLI MVP
description: Implement the agent-ergonomic CLI surface and its self-describing contract. Deterministic versioned JSON envelope with: ok, outcome_kind (success/partial_success/refusal/cancelled/timeout/error, separate from ok and the exit code), command_id, output_contract_id, schema_version, data_source (live/fixture/empty), request_id, query_id, statement_handle, receipt_hash, timing, warnings, safe_next_commands, repair_commands and did_you_mean (Levenshtein) on errors, budget_consumed, redactions_applied. Binary-embedded agent-handbook/robot-docs returns the whole contract in one call (envelope spec, exit-code dictionary, first ~10 commands, error->next-command map, non_goals). capabilities registry per command: input_schema (JSON Schema 2020-12), output_contract_id, error_families, examples, and safety facets (mutates_local_state/provider_network/read_only/sensitive_output) defaulting to non-mutating/non-sensitive. Commands: capabilities, robot-docs/agent-handbook, doctor, selftest (run the no-account testkit fixtures so an agent can verify protocol behavior OFFLINE before any credential exists), profile validate, profile doctor (also surfaces credential-lifetime warnings -- PAT nearing 15-day expiry, JWT exp beyond the 1h cap, OAuth near ~10min -- WITHOUT leaking the secret), catalog scan, catalog graph --mermaid, dataset inspect, dataset profile (column stats/cardinality/null-rate via SQL pushdown APPROX_* aggregates, NOT local compute), dataset describe-operator --jsonschema, query plan, query run, query cancel, receipt show <hash> (look up a content-addressed receipt the connector emitted), export, tui. Output: --json default, --toon opt-in, NDJSON progress on stderr for long queries. Exit codes per plan; empty result is success (exit 0), never exit 1; stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics, no ANSI in JSON. Hold to the agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools polish bar.
acceptance_criteria: deterministic JSON envelope golden per command (including selftest, dataset profile, receipt show); agent-handbook golden covering envelope/exit-codes/non_goals; capabilities registry golden with safety facets for every command; did_you_mean Levenshtein unit tests; error registry auto-populates safe_next_commands/repair_commands for every code (test enumerates all codes); empty-result-is-exit-0 test; selftest runs fully offline and reports fixture pass/fail; receipt show round-trips a stored receipt; dataset profile pushes APPROX_* down (golden SQL, no local stats); profile doctor credential-lifetime warning test (no secret leaked); NO_COLOR/CI/non-TTY tests; CLI/MCP parity assertions live in the e2e bead; structured JSON-line test logs.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan addition 2026-06-24. Three agent-ergonomic verbs added to the CLI MVP scope (all read-only, deterministic --json):

- selftest --json: run the no-account testkit fixtures so an agent can verify the binary's protocol behavior OFFLINE, before any credential exists. High value for autonomous agents trusting the tool.
- receipt show <hash> --json: look up a content-addressed receipt the connector emitted (the plan produces receipts but had no lookup verb; MCP already references receipt lookup -- make it first-class on the CLI too for parity).
- dataset profile <dataset-id> --json: column stats / cardinality / null-rate via SQL pushdown (APPROX_* aggregates), NOT local computation -- reinforces pushdown-first and gives agents a "describe this table" affordance.

All three must satisfy the same envelope/exit-code/safety-facet contract and appear in capabilities + agent-handbook. Add CLI/MCP parity coverage for receipt show and dataset profile.

title: Draft CLI command surface and deterministic envelope skeleton
description: Pane 7 code-first slice for fsnow-agent-ergonomic-cli-mpk: implement the offline command parser/surface draft, deterministic JSON envelope renderer, stable error/exit-code registry, capabilities/agent-handbook/robot-docs surfaces, and --toon output skeleton in crates/franken-snowflake-cli without claiming the blocked parent bead.

title: Build Asupersync-native HTTPS transport for Snowflake SQL API
description: Build the Asupersync-native HTTPS transport (franken-snowflake-http) using the asupersync HTTP/1.1 client + rustls TLS (tls + tls-native-roots), pooled keep-alive across submit/poll/partition GETs. Engineer around the five client realities: gzip is NOT auto-decompressed (apply GzipDecompressor manually off content-encoding); the high-level client takes no injected transport (test via the Http1Client codec seam); the TLS handshake is not cancel-safe (treat cancel-during-connect as a distinct state); the client is HTTP/1.1 only; built-in retry only honors Retry-After (layer jittered exponential backoff on top). Headers: bearer auth, token-type, request id, query tag. No tokio/reqwest/hyper/axum/tower.
acceptance_criteria: unit tests for header construction and bearer/token-type wiring; gzip decompression test on a fixture partition body; jittered-backoff retry test honoring Retry-After then exponential schedule (deterministic clock); cancel-during-connect distinct-state test; body-limit enforcement test; structured JSON-line logs with request fingerprint (redacted) per attempt.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Asupersync is the heavy-lift dependency: networking, TLS path, structured regions, cancellation, retry budgets, statement cancel-on-local-cancel, bounded partition fetchers, deterministic lab tests. Production graph must remain free of Tokio/reqwest/hyper/axum/tower.

title: Implement secret-safe auth model (PAT, OAuth) and JWT signer integration
description: Own the auth model and secret-safe construction (franken-snowflake-auth): the auth trait, secret-source descriptors that reference env var names or external handles (never raw values), PAT bearer headers (X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type: PROGRAMMATIC_ACCESS_TOKEN), and OAuth bearer pass-through. Consume the RS256 key-pair JWT signer from the dedicated JWT crypto bead (w0i.2) rather than reimplementing it. The model must account for credential lifetimes: PAT defaults to a 15-day expiry (policy-capped, max 365, ~15 per user); OAuth access tokens are short-lived (~10 min) and need refresh -- treat a 401 mid-poll as a re-auth trigger; key-pair JWT effective validity is capped at 1 hour. Expose lifetime metadata so profile doctor can warn ("expires in N days/minutes") WITHOUT leaking the secret. Auth values are redacted in Debug/Display/JSON by default; the real env var name is skip_serializing.
acceptance_criteria: PAT and OAuth header-construction unit tests (correct X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type per lane); redaction tests proving no secret appears in Debug/Display/JSON/error text; secret-source resolves env-var presence without reading the value in offline mode; integrates the JWT signer behind the auth trait; credential-lifetime metadata surfaced for doctor warnings (PAT/OAuth/JWT) without leaking the value; OAuth 401-mid-poll re-auth trigger path test; negative test for a missing env var producing a teaching error with the exact next command; structured JSON-line logs.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan correction 2026-06-24 (Snowflake-docs-validated). Credential lifetimes the auth/profile model must account for:

- PAT: defaults to 15-day expiry (policy-capped, max 365), limited to ~15 per user, supports role restriction and network-policy bypass minutes. Header type X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type: PROGRAMMATIC_ACCESS_TOKEN.
- OAuth access tokens are short-lived (~10 min) and need refresh during long-polled queries; treat a 401 mid-poll as a re-auth trigger.
- Key-pair JWT effective validity is capped at 1 hour (see the JWT bead w0i.2).

profile doctor should surface credential-lifetime warnings WITHOUT leaking the secret: "PAT expires in N days", "JWT exp would exceed the 1h cap", "OAuth token near its ~10min lifetime". Goal is advance warning, not a surprise 401 mid-poll.

Refs: docs.snowflake.com programmatic-access-tokens, oauth-snowflake-overview (consulted 2026-06-24).

title: Implement catalog discovery and dataset manifest generation
description: Implement catalog discovery via INFORMATION_SCHEMA and a three-part, independently-queryable dataset model (more robust than inline operator_set fields): (1) a dataset manifest with object, rights class, default/max row limits, and a per-FIELD role assignment from a fixed enum (entity_key/time_index/known_at/feature/label/metadata); (2) a column catalog mapping each column to Snowflake logical type, scale, nullability, and aliases (which power did_you_mean); (3) an operator catalog mapping each operator to input arity, output-dtype rule, and refusal codes. Filters are a dumb predicate AST over column names; operator-vs-dtype legality is checked by a separate validation pass emitting typed refusals. dataset describe-operator --jsonschema projects each operator parameter set to a JSON Schema 2020-12 doc. ACCOUNT_USAGE is a later enhancement.
acceptance_criteria: catalog scan against the mock/fixtures produces deterministic manifest + column catalog + operator catalog goldens; per-field role assignment unit tests; validation-pass tests for operator/dtype legality (positive + typed-refusal negative); describe-operator --jsonschema golden; did_you_mean alias tests; structured JSON-line logs.

comments.author: codex-pane8
comments.text: Pane 8 restart note: docs/AGENTS/README/plan/catalog+graph sections and docs/dataset_manifest_contract.md were read. Agent Mail registration/reservation failed before edits because MCP Agent Mail is in degraded_read_only with db_health/archive_db_parity red; macro_start_session refused writes with 'database disk image is malformed'. One repair attempt also refused: live mailbox owner PID 3110589 is running a deleted am executable; tool classified supervised-owner-required and explicitly said not to force-kill. Because crates/franken-snowflake-catalog and crates/franken-snowflake-graph reservations could not be acquired, I did not edit source files or claim the blocked catalog/graph beads. Named beads remain blocked by upstream dependencies per br show.

title: Implement dataset query planner with safe predicate pushdown
description: Implement the dataset query planner with safe predicate pushdown. Two modes: raw SQL (expert) and dataset mode (agent). HARD RULE: emit Snowflake POSITIONAL TYPED bindings, never string interpolation. Quote identifiers correctly; push down projection and predicates; require a limit or export mode for large result sets; warn on unconstrained queries sized against the Budget cost quota. Compile --as-of to a Time Travel AT(TIMESTAMP=>...) clause when the dataset declares a known_at/time_index axis. Generate deterministic PLAN identifiers (a normalized-plan hash over plan + profile + time window), distinct from Snowflake assigned query_id. Set QUERY_TAG to the envelope command_id/trace_id for end-to-end traceability into Snowflake query history. Support query plan / --dry-run.
acceptance_criteria: planner emits positional typed bindings (no interpolation) verified by golden SQL; identifier-quoting tests incl injection attempts; --as-of compiles to AT(TIMESTAMP=>...) test; unconstrained-query cost warning test; deterministic plan-id (normalized-plan hash) test; QUERY_TAG=trace_id wiring test; dataset-mode and raw-mode dry-run goldens; structured JSON-line logs.

title: Design deferred write-intent mutation ladder
description: Design but do not implement broad mutation support. Future write operations must use write plan --dry-run, statement allowlists, exact confirmation tokens, requestId idempotency, append-only audit records, and narrow first surfaces such as insert into staging tables or merge by explicit key manifest. Acceptance: design doc makes read-only MVP clear and prevents accidental DDL/DML enablement.
acceptance_criteria: write-intent ladder specified and tested in dry-run only: write plan --dry-run, typed safety classification, statement allowlist, idempotency request id, exact confirmation token, execution receipt, append-only audit; the ladder is the ONLY path that may request a capability row wider than read-only (compile/test enforced); DDL refused; no live mutation executed in tests; structured logs.

title: Build deterministic no-account Snowflake SQL API testkit
description: Build the no-account testkit in two lanes. PRIMARY deterministic lane (no socket): drive the Http1Client codec over a VirtualTcpStream pair under LabRuntime with scripted responses; canned fixtures for 200 result sets, 202 running, 429 backoff, 422 failure, gzip partitions, multiple-statement refusal. INTEGRATION lane: a fastapi_rust mock SQL API server (Asupersync-native, dev-dependency only) for stateful CLI<->HTTP flows (first GET 202, later GET 200 with partitions, gzip bodies, custom headers). Shared infra: key-sorted deterministic-json goldens with canonicalized time/host/hash fields and IEEE-754 bits on float mismatch; injected clock; canary-secret leak guards; forbidden-dependency scan. The DPOR race suite is a separate bead built on this lane.
acceptance_criteria: each fixture has a passing replay test on the primary lane; the fastapi_rust mock serves the 202->200 progression and gzip partitions in an integration test; golden canonicalization verified; canary-secret guard fails on a planted secret; auth-header redaction inspected; structured JSON-line test logs; cargo test stays fully offline by default.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: fastapi_rust is most relevant here as a deterministic mock Snowflake SQL API server or local dev harness. Keep it testkit/dev-scoped unless a later design proves a production need.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan addition 2026-06-24. Extra no-account fixtures the testkit should ship (beyond the existing 200/202/429/422/gzip/multi-statement-refusal set):

- Status-code routing proving 200/202/408/422/429 are distinct: a 202->200 poll-continue sequence; a 429 backoff with NO Retry-After header; a 408 statement-timeout outcome; a 422 failure -- none conflated.
- Idempotent-resubmit fixture: requestId + retry=true returns the prior result rather than re-executing.
- JWT claim-format golden: iss=ACCOUNT.USER.SHA256:<fp>, sub=ACCOUNT.USER (no fp), uppercasing, org-account "."->"-", exp<=3600 cap.
- jsonv2 wire-codec unit tests: DATE (epoch days), TIME/TIMESTAMP_* (fractional epoch seconds), TIMESTAMP_TZ (offset+1440), NUMBER (no scale division), BOOLEAN (string).
- Multi-statement (when allowed): MULTI_STATEMENT_COUNT + statementHandles[] fan-out fixture, plus a bindings-with-multi-statement refusal.
- Cancelled-during-connect receipt-state fixture (TLS handshake not cancel-safe).
- Add fixed-seed chaos presets (asupersync with_light_chaos) on the 429-storm and partial-partition-failure DPOR suites, and assert the obligation-leak + quiescence oracles as CI GATES (not informational).

title: Specify and test downstream integration contract
description: Write public downstream adapter contract fixtures without naming private consumers: connector manifest shape, profile diagnostics, refusal codes, dataset manifest import, content-addressed result receipts, frame ingest, adapter fixtures, rights metadata, and output contract discipline. Acceptance: docs and fixtures let downstream projects consume franken_snowflake outputs without embedding Snowflake protocol code.
acceptance_criteria: a narrow public adapter trait is specified and contract-tested against fixtures: provider/profile diagnostics, catalog discovery, dataset manifests, query receipts, content-addressed exports; the contract exposes the same envelopes/error codes/safety classes as the CLI/MCP; adapter-fixtures feature builds; no private/downstream names anywhere (public-safety scan); structured JSON-line logs.

title: Add optional FrankenPandas frame materialization
description: Add optional frame materialization (franken-snowflake-frame) driven by resultSetMetaData.rowType (NOT row inspection). The jsonv2 WIRE CODEC is where dtype bugs hide and is DISTINCT from the destination dtype mapping: every cell is a JSON STRING parsed per its rowType (case-insensitive), never inferred from JSON shape -- NUMBER/FIXED plain decimal (do NOT divide by 10^scale); REAL/FLOAT numeric string (DECFLOAT scientific past 38 digits); BOOLEAN "true"/"false"; DATE days-since-epoch; TIME/TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ fractional epoch SECONDS (not nanos); TIMESTAMP_TZ "<epoch_sec.frac> <offset>" with offset = minutes+1440; BINARY hex; VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY embedded JSON. Pin and test the destination Snowflake-type -> frame-dtype mapping: FIXED scale0 -> Int64/nullable; FIXED scale>0 and REAL -> Float64; TEXT -> Utf8; BOOLEAN -> Bool/nullable; DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP_* -> Datetime64 (lossy: carry the Snowflake logical type alongside the frame dtype); VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY -> structured JSON (Utf8); BINARY -> Binary/hex. Use FrankenPandas fp-columnar + fp-types (fp-frame only for full DataFrame semantics); NEVER the umbrella crate and NEVER fp-io (its non-optional orc-rust pulls Tokio -- note the sql-* features are SYNC drivers and are NOT the leak). Stream large partitions; separate null/NaN/NaT. The core protocol client must not require this crate. The ambiguous timestamp unit is pinned by the empirical live golden (bead w0i.13).
acceptance_criteria: jsonv2 wire-codec unit tests per type: DATE (epoch days), TIME/TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ (fractional epoch seconds), TIMESTAMP_TZ (offset+1440), NUMBER/FIXED (no 10^scale division), BOOLEAN (string), BINARY (hex), DECFLOAT (scientific); destination dtype-mapping unit test for every row covering the pinned table; VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY preserved as structured JSON test; DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP logical-type-retained test; null/NaN/NaT separation test; streaming materialization of a multi-partition fixture; cargo-tree proof the fp-* path pulls no forbidden deps AND fp-io is absent from the graph; structured JSON-line logs.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan correction 2026-06-24. Two things for frame materialization:

1) Dependency: depend on fp-columnar + fp-types (+ fp-frame only when full DataFrame semantics are needed). Do NOT pull fp-io -- it pulls Tokio non-optionally via orc-rust (see export bead w0i.8). The core protocol client must not require frame materialization at all.

2) jsonv2 wire codec (this is where dtype bugs hide). The Snowflake-type -> frame-dtype table is the DESTINATION mapping; it is distinct from decoding the wire. Every data cell is a JSON STRING parsed by its rowType (case-insensitive), never inferred from JSON shape:
- NUMBER/FIXED: plain decimal string; do NOT divide by 10^scale.
- REAL/FLOAT: numeric string; DECFLOAT -> scientific past 38 digits.
- BOOLEAN: the strings "true"/"false".
- DATE: days-since-epoch (e.g. "18262").
- TIME/TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ: fractional epoch SECONDS (e.g. "82919.000000000"), not nanos.
- TIMESTAMP_TZ: "<epoch_sec.frac> <offset>", offset = minutes+1440.
- BINARY: hex; VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY: preserve embedded JSON.
Docs are inconsistent on timestamp units; pin the codec with an empirical live golden (see the new live-golden bead). Acceptance: per-type wire-codec unit tests for the above.

title: Create opt-in live trial and credential proof lanes
description: Define live proof lanes that are skipped/refused without credentials: trial account smoke, profile doctor, minimal select, catalog scan, async cancel, and partitioned result query. Live tests must never silently pass without credentials and must record docs date, account/profile redaction, and exact proof command. Acceptance: scripts and docs make live proof repeatable once a Snowflake account exists.
acceptance_criteria: live tests gated on explicit credentials; absent creds yield a TYPED skip outcome with evidence (never silent pass); spawned-CLI helper strips secret env vars and disables live transport from the other side; covers profile doctor --online, catalog scan, small select, async long-running query + cancel, partitioned result; each records exact proof artifacts; structured JSON-line logs.

title: Add FrankenSQLite and sqlmodel_rust local cache/repository layer
description: Add the FrankenSQLite-backed local cache/repository layer (franken-snowflake-cache) via the sqlmodel-frankensqlite driver (NOT the C-FFI sqlmodel-sqlite). Repositories for: profiles without secrets, catalog snapshots, dataset manifests, query plans, query receipts (content-addressed BLAKE3), result partition metadata, cost/row-count history, offline replay bundles, and an APPEND-ONLY query audit log enforced by a build-failing test that forbids any UPDATE/DELETE. Concurrency reality: fsqlite::Connection is !Send and access is blocking-under-mutex (fine for a read-mostly local cache); last_insert_rowid is adapter-tracked. Migrations are explicit and versioned.
acceptance_criteria: repository CRUD unit tests per entity; receipt content-addressing + byte-length verification test; append-only audit-log build-failing UPDATE/DELETE gate test; migration up/down test; secret-free profile storage test; concurrency note exercised by a serialized-access test; structured JSON-line logs.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Use FrankenSQLite for durable local metadata and query receipts; use sqlmodel_rust only where typed local models make the repository layer clearer. Never store raw Snowflake secrets in this cache.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text: Cross-platform 2026-06-24: WINDOWS COMPILE BLOCKER (upstream prerequisite). fsqlite currently declares the Unix-only `nix` crate under [target.'cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))'.dependencies] in crates/fsqlite-vfs/Cargo.toml and crates/fsqlite-mvcc/Cargo.toml -- that cfg is TRUE on Windows, so Windows pulls `nix` and fails to compile even though every nix:: call site is cfg(unix)-gated. fsqlite DOES have a real Windows VFS (LockFileEx path in fsqlite-vfs/src/windows.rs), so the logic is fine; only the dependency gating is wrong. Fix upstream in frankensqlite: re-gate `nix` to [target.'cfg(unix)'.dependencies] in both crates, and add a full-workspace Windows build to fsqlite CI (its current windows-vfs-interop.yml only checks -p fsqlite-vfs). This must land before the cache crate targets Windows. Tracked in the cross-platform bead.

title: Build franken_snowflake native Snowflake connector
description: Build /dp/franken_snowflake as a clean-room, Asupersync-native Snowflake SQL API connector and agent-ergonomic CLI. The project must be independently useful as an open-source FrankenSuite library. Core constraints: no production Tokio/reqwest/hyper/axum/tower/sqlx/diesel/sea-orm, no third-party Rust Snowflake driver dependency, no secrets in repo/logs/fixtures, read/query/catalog first, write support deferred behind explicit write-intent safety. Success means a no-account testkit, stable CLI JSON contracts, Snowflake SQL API statement lifecycle support, profile/auth handling, catalog/dataset manifest model, and public downstream adapter fixtures.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Plan source: COMPREHENSIVE_PLAN_FOR_FRANKEN_SNOWFLAKE.md. Future agents should treat that plan plus AGENTS.md as the repo contract, then use this Beads graph for execution. Core direction: Asupersync-native Snowflake SQL API client and agent CLI first; public downstream adapter fixtures only after protocol, auth, CLI, and no-account testkit are stable.

title: Ship feature-gated MCP server surface (franken-snowflake mcp serve)
description: Ship the feature-gated MCP server surface (franken-snowflake mcp serve) on fastmcp-rust, exposing each READ verb (query run/plan, catalog scan, dataset inspect, dataset profile, doctor, capabilities, receipt show) as an MCP tool whose JSON schema is generated from the handler signature. It is NOT a second product contract: it shares the CLI command handlers and therefore the same envelopes, error codes, receipts, and safety classes; the MCP crate is a thin adapter, not a parallel implementation. Sequencing: run_stdio + read-only tools FIRST (the agent-spawn / Claude Desktop path), run_http SECOND once stdio is proven, write tools deferred behind the same write-intent ladder as the CLI. Wrap each call in an Asupersync web::request_region so an agent disconnect drains the statement bracket PLUS the partition Scope as one owned region (not merely cooperative checkpoint points); ctx.checkpoint() provides the cancel points inside the region (this matters most for run_http). Gate behind an mcp feature so the leanest agent build can omit it. fastmcp-rust is a candidate dep until cargo-tree-proven.
acceptance_criteria: MCP tool-schema golden fixture; CLI/MCP parity test (same op -> identical envelope/error code/receipt/safety class through both surfaces); request_region disconnect-mid-query test that fires the remote cancel endpoint and leaves zero orphaned statements/partition fetchers (obligation-leak oracle); read-only capability posture enforced; stdio path proven before http; cargo-tree proof fastmcp-rust pulls no forbidden deps; structured JSON-line logs.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan addition 2026-06-24 (asupersync-verified). Strengthen MCP cancellation beyond checkpoint():

Wrap each MCP/HTTP serve call in an asupersync web::request_region (asupersync/src/web/request_region.rs) so an agent disconnect drains the statement bracket PLUS the partition Scope as one owned region -- not merely cooperative ctx.checkpoint() points (checkpoint provides the cancel points INSIDE the region). This is the skill's headline request-as-region pattern (WEB-GRPC-HTTP.md) and applies especially to run_http(). Acceptance: a disconnect-mid-query test asserting the remote cancel endpoint fires and no statement/partition fetcher is orphaned (obligation-leak oracle).

title: Add --toon token-efficient output mode alongside --json on read commands
description: Integrate the toon (token-oriented object notation) encoder so every read command accepts --toon as an alternative to --json, cutting token cost on large catalog and result payloads fed back to agents. --toon must round-trip to the same logical data as --json (golden parity test). Default output stays --json; --toon is an explicit opt-in flag, available by default in the build. Integral default capability.
acceptance_criteria: every read command emits valid --toon; --toon round-trips to the same logical data as --json (parity golden); token-count regression check shows --toon smaller than --json on a large payload; --json remains the default; cargo-tree admissibility proof; structured logs.

title: Build optional human-facing TUI on FrankenTUI (catalog browser, query runner, live progress)
description: franken-snowflake-tui: a DEFAULT-OFF / OPT-IN human-facing TUI behind a `tui` feature, built on FrankenTUI (Asupersync-native + crossterm; pin the crossterm-compat feature for Windows; never charmed_rust, which pulls Tokio). It stays first-class but is NOT in the default build until its cargo-tree admissibility AND Windows cross-platform proofs are boring -- it is the heaviest, most platform-sensitive (crossterm-compat/Windows console), and least agent-relevant surface, so it must not gate or bloat the default agent binary before the core connector is real. Catalog browser (navigate databases->schemas->tables->columns; needs catalog discovery), interactive query runner reusing the same planner/safety/receipts as the CLI (needs the dataset planner), and live statement + partition-fetch progress driven by the same Asupersync Scope/Budget a query uses. Presentation layer over the shared handlers; introduces no new product contract, secrets handling, or mutation path. Promoting it to default-on later is a one-line feature change once the proofs are green.
acceptance_criteria: TUI model/update unit tests over scripted catalog state with no real terminal; query runner reuses the shared planner/receipts (parity with CLI); catalog browser reflects catalog-discovery output; live statement+partition progress driven by the query Scope/Budget verified against a mock; not-charmed_rust enforced by cargo-tree (no tokio); the DEFAULT build EXCLUDES the tui feature (default-features build test), the tui feature builds and runs, and a Windows build succeeds via crossterm-compat; structured logs.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text: Cross-platform 2026-06-24: FrankenTUI has two backends -- a native Unix-only termios path (ftui-tty, nix-based) and a cross-platform fallback on crossterm 0.29 behind the `crossterm-compat` feature. run_native() returns Unsupported on non-Unix, so WINDOWS portability requires enabling `crossterm-compat`. Pin frankentui with the crossterm-compat feature in franken_snowflake's Cargo.toml, and CI-build the TUI on windows-latest. macOS is fine (gets the Unix backend). Tracked in the cross-platform bead.

title: Build comprehensive e2e + integration test harness with structured logging
description: Runnable end-to-end harness that drives the REAL CLI binary and the MCP serve surface against the fastapi_rust mock server through full flows: profile validate -> catalog scan -> catalog graph -> dataset inspect -> dataset profile -> query plan -> query run (202->200 with gzip partitions) -> query cancel -> receipt show -> export, plus an offline selftest run. Provide BOTH a PORTABLE runner (a cargo xtask e2e or a pure-Rust integration test) so Windows CI runs the full flow WITHOUT bash, AND scripts/e2e/*.sh as the Unix convenience wrapper -- keep both, do not drop either. The runner/scripts are idempotent and hermetic (secret env vars stripped, live transport disabled) and exit non-zero on any deviation from the expected envelope/exit-code. Every step emits structured JSON-line logs (trace_id, command_id, step name, timing, outcome, and expected-vs-actual on failure) to a per-run artifacts directory so a failed run is legible and replayable without re-instrumentation, plus a human-readable run summary at the end. Includes a CLI/MCP parity check (same op -> identical envelope/error code/receipt/safety class through both surfaces) and a coverage floor enforced in CI. Deterministic clock + fixed seeds; canonicalization zeroes time/host/hash fields before golden compare and reports IEEE-754 bits on float mismatch. Builds on the shared test+observability harness.
acceptance_criteria: a portable runner (cargo xtask e2e / pure-Rust integration test) AND scripts/e2e/*.sh both drive the real CLI binary and mcp serve against the fastapi_rust mock through the full flow (profile validate -> catalog scan -> catalog graph -> dataset inspect -> dataset profile -> query plan -> query run 202->200 gzip partitions -> query cancel -> receipt show -> export -> selftest); idempotent, hermetic (secrets stripped, live transport off), exit non-zero on any envelope/exit-code deviation; CLI/MCP parity asserted; per-step structured JSON-line logs (trace_id/command_id/step/timing/outcome + expected-vs-actual on failure) to a per-run artifacts dir plus a human-readable summary; deterministic clock + fixed seeds; golden canonicalization with IEEE-754 bits on float mismatch; coverage floor enforced in CI; the portable runner passes on the Windows CI matrix.

title: Capture empirical jsonv2 result-encoding live golden
description:
Capture an empirically verified golden of the Snowflake SQL API jsonv2 result-data encoding from a real response, because the official docs are internally inconsistent on timestamp units (one passage says nanoseconds; the per-type table implies fractional epoch seconds). Run a small SELECT covering every wire type (NUMBER with scale, REAL/FLOAT, DECFLOAT, BOOLEAN, DATE, TIME, TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ/TZ at known instants, BINARY, VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY, NULL) against a trial account, capture the exact data-array strings, and pin them as the authoritative golden that the frame wire-codec (bead z1o) and protocol schemas (bead kx6) are validated against. Redact account/host; the golden carries only column types and the literal encoded strings.

Confirm specifically: DATE = days-since-epoch; TIME/TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ = fractional epoch SECONDS (not nanos); TIMESTAMP_TZ offset = minutes+1440; NUMBER decimal not scale-divided; BOOLEAN as "true"/"false". If any differs from the assumed codec, fix the codec and re-run.

Ref: docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/sql-api/handling-responses (consulted 2026-06-24).
acceptance_criteria: golden file of literal jsonv2 data-array strings for every wire type captured from a live trial-account response; timestamp unit (fractional epoch seconds vs nanos) empirically confirmed and the frame codec asserted against it; account/host redacted; live test skips with a typed outcome when no credentials present.

title: Establish cross-platform support (Linux/macOS/Windows): CI matrix, config dirs, CRLF goldens, portable e2e
description:
Make franken_snowflake fully cross-platform with Linux, macOS, and Windows as tier-1 targets. This is feasible because Asupersync is portable (epoll/kqueue/IOCP via the polling crate; io_uring is optional, NOT required; its CI builds+tests all three OSes with the tls/tls-native-roots/compression feature set the connector uses) and the HTTP/TLS/gzip client path has no platform-specific code. The work is discipline, not a runtime port.

Scope:
- CI OS matrix: a test job os=[ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest], fail-fast=false, building the FULL workspace with --features tls,tls-native-roots,compression (do not test only one sub-crate on Windows).
- Portable pins: rustls + rustls-native-certs; flate2 default miniz_oxide backend (NEVER the zlib/zlib-ng C backends); jsonwebtoken rust_crypto+use_pem; frankentui with crossterm-compat (its native termios backend is Unix-only). Document the ring C/asm build-toolchain prerequisite (MSVC on Windows).
- UPSTREAM PREREQUISITE: fix the fsqlite Windows compile blocker -- re-gate the `nix` crate from cfg(not(wasm32)) to cfg(unix) in fsqlite-vfs and fsqlite-mvcc; add a full-workspace Windows build to fsqlite CI.
- Config/cache/artifacts dirs via directories/dirs ProjectDirs (XDG / ~/Library / %APPDATA%); precedence explicit-flag > env > platform default; no hard-coded Unix paths.
- Golden-file newline discipline: .gitattributes forcing eol=lf on *.json/*.golden/*.toml; compare goldens as raw bytes; CI check that no golden contains "\r"; lowercase non-case-colliding fixture filenames (case-insensitive FS on Windows/macOS).
- Portable e2e: ensure the portable e2e runner (owned by the e2e bead w0i.12, cargo xtask / pure-Rust) executes in the Windows CI matrix; document the Git-Bash/WSL fallback for the Unix .sh wrappers.
- Ctrl+C -> CancelReason / CancelKind::User mapping, portable (signal-hook on Unix; a windows-sys console-ctrl handler or the ctrlc crate on Windows). TTY/NO_COLOR via IsTerminal (already portable).
acceptance_criteria: full-workspace build + no-account testkit green on ubuntu/macos/windows CI (fail-fast=false); fsqlite nix re-gated to cfg(unix) and Windows workspace build passes; config/cache/artifacts dirs resolve per-OS via ProjectDirs with documented precedence; .gitattributes forces eol=lf and a CI check rejects \r in goldens; cargo xtask e2e runs the full flow on Windows CI without bash; Ctrl+C maps to CancelKind::User on all three OSes; frankentui builds on Windows via crossterm-compat.

title: Build shared test + observability harness (golden framework, JSON-line logger, deterministic clock, canary guard)
description: Build the shared, reusable test + observability harness used by every crate's unit tests and the testkit/DPOR/e2e lanes, so "comprehensive tests with detailed logging" is implemented ONCE rather than re-invented per bead. It provides: (1) a deterministic golden framework -- key-sorted JSON, canonicalization that zeroes time/host/hash fields, byte-exact comparison, and IEEE-754-bit reporting on float mismatch; (2) a structured JSON-line test logger emitting one event per step (trace_id, command_id, step, timing, outcome, and expected-vs-actual on failure) to a per-run artifacts directory, plus a human-readable run summary; (3) an injected/deterministic clock for backoff/TTL with fixed seeds; (4) canary-secret leak guards (planted fake-but-detectable secrets) that scan all output channels (stdout/stderr/files); (5) cross-platform golden discipline helpers (assert no "\r" in goldens; eol=lf expectation). It is generic (no Snowflake protocol knowledge and no live network); the Snowflake fixtures and fastapi_rust mock build on top in the testkit bead. Every other crate consumes it as a dev-dependency; new beads must use it rather than hand-rolling logging or golden comparison.
acceptance_criteria: golden framework canonicalizes volatile fields and reports IEEE-754 bits on a deliberate float mismatch (self-test); JSON-line logger emits per-step events (trace_id/command_id/timing/outcome + expected-vs-actual on failure) to a per-run artifacts dir and prints a human-readable summary; injected clock + fixed seeds make a sample backoff schedule reproducible; canary-secret guard fails on a planted secret across stdout/stderr/file outputs; no-"\r" golden assertion helper works; the harness builds and runs on Linux/macOS/Windows and a cargo-tree proof shows it pulls no forbidden deps; structured JSON-line logs.

title: Implement pure-Rust RS256 key-pair JWT signing and public-key fingerprint
description: Concrete forbidden-dependency-free crypto path for Snowflake key-pair auth. Use jsonwebtoken v10 with default-features=false, features=[rust_crypto,use_pem] (pure-Rust rsa+sha2 RS256; no OpenSSL, no ring signing, no Tokio). Load a PKCS#8 PEM private key (passphrase-protected supported) and sign an RS256 JWT with the EXACT Snowflake claim set: iss = "<ACCOUNT>.<USER>.SHA256:<fp>" (WITH the public-key fingerprint), sub = "<ACCOUNT>.<USER>" (NO fingerprint), iat, exp. ACCOUNT and USER are UPPERCASE; for the org-account form whose account segment contains a ".", replace it with "-". Fingerprint = SHA256:<base64(SHA-256 of the DER public key)> via sha2+base64. Snowflake caps effective JWT validity at 1 hour regardless of the requested exp, so cap exp at <=3600s AND support re-signing INSIDE the statement bracket so a query polled past ~1h does not lose auth mid-flight. Set X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type: KEYPAIR_JWT and the bearer header. Precedent: this exact jsonwebtoken v10 rust_crypto+use_pem path already ships in fastmcp-server's jwt feature -- cite that, NOT mcp_agent_mail_rust (which uses the aws_lc_rs C-backed feature; do not copy it). Ref: docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/sql-api/authenticating (2026-06-24).
acceptance_criteria: RS256 sign+verify round-trip golden using a test PKCS#8 key; golden asserting the claim formats differ (iss = ACCOUNT.USER.SHA256:<fp> but sub = ACCOUNT.USER with no fp); uppercase + org-account "."->"-" account normalization test; SHA256:base64(DER pubkey) fingerprint matches the Snowflake format on a known key; exp<=3600 cap test; mid-flight re-sign path test (token re-issued inside a simulated long poll); X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type=KEYPAIR_JWT header set; passphrase-protected key load test; no secret in Debug/JSON (redaction test); cargo-tree proof jsonwebtoken rust_crypto pulls no openssl/ring-signing/tokio; structured JSON-line logs.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan correction 2026-06-24 (Snowflake-docs-validated). JWT claim format in the draft was wrong:

- iss = "<ACCOUNT>.<USER>.SHA256:<fp>" (WITH fingerprint), but sub = "<ACCOUNT>.<USER>" (NO fingerprint). The draft said both carry the fingerprint.
- ACCOUNT and USER must be UPPERCASE. If the account identifier uses the org-account form containing a ".", replace it with "-" in the account segment.
- Fingerprint is SHA256:<base64(SHA-256 of DER public key)>.
- Snowflake caps effective JWT validity at 1 hour regardless of the requested exp. Cap exp at <=3600s and support re-signing INSIDE the statement bracket so a query polled past ~1h does not lose auth mid-flight.

Precedent: this exact jsonwebtoken v10 rust_crypto+use_pem path already ships in fastmcp-server (its jwt feature) -- cite that, NOT mcp_agent_mail_rust (which uses the aws_lc_rs C-backed feature; do not copy it).

Acceptance additions: golden asserting iss/sub formats differ as above; uppercase + org-account dot->dash normalization test; exp<=3600 cap test; mid-flight re-sign path test.

Ref: docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/sql-api/authenticating (consulted 2026-06-24).

title: Pin toolchain and unify Asupersync via patch with a single-version CI gate
description: Pin the toolchain and unify the dependency graph. rust-toolchain.toml (nightly, edition 2024). Workspace lints (forbid unsafe; deny unwrap/expect/panic/todo/dbg). A [patch.crates-io] block unifying asupersync, fsqlite/fsqlite-*, sqlmodel-*, fp-*, fastmcp-rust, frankensearch, frankentui, frankenmermaid, fnx-*, toon to one local checkout set. A CI gate that fails if cargo tree reports more than one asupersync version. The broader per-dependency forbidden-dep admissibility harness is the dependency-admissibility bead, which this bead unblocks.
acceptance_criteria: cargo tree shows exactly one asupersync version (gate test); workspace lints enforced in CI; patch block resolves the whole graph; gate fails on an injected second asupersync version; structured CI logs.
notes: Concrete version skew to resolve during unification: asupersync HEAD is 0.3.5 but fastmcp-rust hard-pins asupersync =0.3.4 and frankensqlite uses 0.3.4. The single-version gate must pick one target (bump fastmcp to 0.3.5 or pin the whole graph to 0.3.4) and patch the graph accordingly; the gate fails the build until exactly one version resolves.

title: Add VirtualTcp + Lab/DPOR deterministic cancel and retry race suite
description: Primary deterministic test lane with no real socket: drive Http1Client::request<IO> codec over a VirtualTcpStream pair under LabRuntime, exploring interleavings with DPOR. Cases: cancel-during-submit, cancel-during-poll, cancel-during-partition-fetch, 429-storm backoff, partial-partition-failure. Each asserts obligation-leak and quiescence oracles: zero leaked connections, statements, or partition fetchers after cancel, and the remote cancel endpoint always fires. Inject a deterministic clock for backoff/TTL.
acceptance_criteria: DPOR explores cancel-during-submit, cancel-during-poll, cancel-during-partition-fetch, 429-storm, partial-partition-failure; each interleaving asserts obligation-leak and quiescence oracles (zero leaked connections/statements/fetchers); remote cancel always fires after submit; deterministic clock + fixed seeds make runs reproducible; a failing interleaving emits a replayable crashpack/manifest; structured JSON-line logs per schedule.

title: Add compile-time credential Debug-leak gate test
description: Build-failing test that scans crate sources and fails if any #[derive(Debug)] struct has a credential-shaped field (*_api_key, *_password, *_private_key, *_token, *_pat, ...) without a hand-rolled redacting Debug. Pair with one composable redactor sourcing its needle list from a single shared constant (shared with the last-mile output scanner) and longest-prefix secret detection (eyJ/AKIA/ghp_/sk-/xoxb-/glpat-/AIza/...). Cheapest insurance against the most common secret leak; critical since we handle RSA keys and PATs.
acceptance_criteria: build-failing test scans sources and rejects any derive(Debug) struct with a credential-shaped field (_api_key/_password/_private_key/_token/_pat/...) lacking a hand-rolled redacting Debug; positive control (a properly redacted struct passes) and negative control (a leaky struct fails); shared needle list reused by the redactor; structured logs.

title: Adopt Asupersync Budget/Outcome/CancelReason/capability semantics in core
description: Adopt the Asupersync Budget/Outcome/CancelKind/capability control plane in franken-snowflake-core and thread it to the edges. Build SnowflakeOutcome<T> on the four-valued Outcome (Ok/Err/Cancelled/Panicked) and preserve all four to the CLI/MCP edge, collapsing only at the policy boundary (exit code/MCP error/receipt status). Own the envelope contract types (OutcomeKind, DataSource), the error code ranges, and the central error registry mapping each code to default safe_next_commands/repair_commands. Model query Budget with deadline + poll quota + cost quota and meet() propagation to partition fetchers; the cost quota is ADVISORY (the enforceable ceiling is server-side STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_IN_SECONDS + result row caps) and a breach surfaces as Cancelled(CancelKind::CostBudget). Cancellation policy keys off reason.kind: CancelKind -- CancelReason is a STRUCT, not a flat enum (asupersync/src/types/cancel.rs). Route Deadline/CostBudget (budget exhaustion) and Timeout (explicit timeout combinator) to retry/degrade, User to remote-cancel + receipt, Shutdown to bounded drain, RaceLost/ParentCancelled to quiet drain. Read-only is enforced at the type level AT THE RIGHT LAYER: the pure planning/validation/SQL-compile path runs under cx_readonly() (= Cx<cap::None>, zero capabilities including NO IO); the transport layer runs under a narrowed Cx granting IO (+TIME/SPAWN) but NEVER REMOTE; only the write-intent ladder widens authority further. See docs/asupersync_leverage.md.
acceptance_criteria: unit tests that all four Outcome states survive to the envelope and map to correct exit codes (empty result = exit 0); error-registry completeness test (every code has a default recovery path); Budget meet() child-tightening propagation test; reason.kind / CancelKind -> policy mapping tests with Deadline and CostBudget routed distinctly from Timeout; a compile-time/test assertion that the pure planner layer holds cx_readonly() (no IO) while the transport layer holds a narrowed Cx with IO+TIME+SPAWN but NOT REMOTE, and that only the write-intent ladder can widen further; CostBudget breach maps to a distinct outcome_kind/exit code; structured JSON-line logs.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan correction 2026-06-24 (verified against /data/projects/asupersync source):

- CancelReason is a STRUCT, not a flat enum. Match reason.kind: CancelKind (asupersync/src/types/cancel.rs). A budgeted query that runs out of time surfaces as CancelKind::Deadline (and a cost-quota breach as CancelKind::CostBudget), NOT Timeout (which is the explicit timeout-combinator path). The policy table must route Deadline/CostBudget explicitly (retry or degrade). Update the acceptance criterion accordingly.
- cx_readonly() returns Cx<cap::None> -- ZERO capabilities, including no IO (asupersync/src/cx/cap.rs). So the acceptance criterion "read handlers cannot obtain IO/REMOTE/SPAWN" is wrong for the transport layer: the network read path DOES need a narrowed Cx with IO (and TIME/SPAWN) but never REMOTE. cx_readonly() fits only the pure planning/validation/SQL-compile layer. Re-word the read-only enforcement test: planner layer = cx_readonly(); transport layer = narrowed Cx with IO, REMOTE off; only the write-intent ladder widens further.
- cost quota is advisory; enforceable ceiling is server-side STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_IN_SECONDS + row caps. A CostBudget breach maps to a distinct outcome_kind/exit code.
- The submit POST is NOT retried by the client RetryPolicy (non-idempotent methods excluded). Our jittered backoff owns submit retries, made safe by requestId + retry=true.

See docs/asupersync_leverage.md (updated 2026-06-24).

title: Prove per-dependency admissibility with cargo-tree forbidden-dep scans
description: Treat every non-asupersync FrankenSuite dependency as a CANDIDATE until proven. Build a per-crate cargo-tree harness that fails CI if tokio/reqwest/hyper/axum/tower/sqlx/diesel/sea-orm appear in the production feature graph a given dep contributes. Cover the admissible candidates: fastmcp-rust, fp-columnar/fp-types/fp-frame (NOT fp-io), jsonwebtoken, fastapi-rust (dev-only), frankensearch (hash/lexical), the optional frankensearch-rerank `native` + frankentorch ft-* crates (rerank feature), frankentui, frankenmermaid, the FrankenNetworkX fnx-* crates, and toon. fp-io is NOT a candidate -- it is FORBIDDEN because its non-optional orc-rust dependency pulls Tokio -- so the harness must include a NEGATIVE gate asserting fp-io, orc-rust, and tokio are ABSENT from every production feature graph (especially the frame and export crates), not merely scanning fp-io as if it were admissible. Dev-only and feature-gated paths are scanned in their own configs so a dev-only tokio can never reach production. The harness must be extended whenever a new dependency is introduced.
acceptance_criteria: a cargo-tree harness runs per candidate dep and fails on any tokio/reqwest/hyper/axum/tower/sqlx/diesel/sea-orm in its production feature graph; a NEGATIVE gate asserts fp-io / orc-rust / tokio are ABSENT from the frame and export production graphs; dev-only and feature-gated configs scanned separately; a deliberately-misconfigured feature that leaks tokio (e.g. enabling fp-io) is caught by the test; the harness documents how to extend it for a new dep; structured CI logs listing each dep verdict.

title: Build content-addressed result export crate (COPY INTO + local CSV/JSONL)
description: franken-snowflake-export: COPY INTO <location> is the PRIMARY large-export path -- server-side, pushes the heavy work to Snowflake, pulls no heavy local deps, and aligns with the pushdown-first philosophy. Local export is limited to CSV and JSONL, written by hand or via fp-columnar ALONE. Do NOT depend on fp-io: its non-optional orc-rust dependency pulls Tokio and would break the forbidden-dependency gate (note the sql-* features pull SYNC drivers and are NOT the leak). ORC export is dropped entirely (it is the Tokio vector and is unrequested). Local Arrow IPC is added only if a forbidden-dependency-clean writer is cargo-tree-proven; otherwise it is deferred in favor of COPY INTO. Emit content-addressed (BLAKE3) export records with byte-length verification. Stream large result sets rather than buffering. The export verb is always present; the local-file/columnar backends are pulled only when the export feature is enabled.
acceptance_criteria: local CSV and JSONL export goldens from a fixture result set; a COPY INTO <location> export-plan generation golden (the primary large-export path); content-addressed (BLAKE3) record with byte-length verification test; export feature off -> local backends absent AND fp-io absent from the production graph (cargo-tree); large-result streaming test (no full buffering); structured JSON-line logs with the export record hash.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan correction 2026-06-24 (verified via cargo-tree on /data/projects/frankenpandas): do NOT depend on fp-io.

fp-io has a NON-optional dependency on orc-rust 0.8.0, which pulls Tokio (cargo tree -i tokio: tokio -> orc-rust -> fp-io -> frankenpandas). So the planned "Arrow/Parquet through fp-io/fp-columnar" breaks the forbidden-dependency gate. Note the draft blamed the sql-* features, but those pull SYNC drivers (rusqlite/postgres/mysql) and are not the leak; the leak is orc-rust in fp-io.

Revised export design:
- COPY INTO <location> is the PRIMARY large-export path (server-side, no heavy local deps; aligns with pushdown-first).
- Local export limited to CSV and JSONL, written by hand or via fp-columnar ALONE (no fp-io).
- ORC export dropped entirely (it is the Tokio vector and unrequested).
- Local Arrow IPC only if a forbidden-dep-clean writer is cargo-tree-proven; else defer in favor of COPY INTO.

Acceptance update: replace the CSV/Arrow/Parquet goldens with CSV/JSONL goldens + a COPY INTO export-plan golden; keep the "export feature off -> backends absent" cargo-tree check and add an "fp-io absent from the graph" assertion.

title: Model catalog as a lineage graph with algorithms and Mermaid/SVG output
description: franken-snowflake-graph: model databases->schemas->tables/views->columns plus FK, view-dependency, and lineage edges as a typed graph. Provide agent-discovery algorithms: what-relates-to <object>, ancestors/descendants (lineage up/down), reachability, dependency-cycle detection. Build on the FrankenNetworkX Rust crates (fnx-classes/fnx-algorithms/...) via path dep; if their internal API proves unstable, fall back to a thin in-house adjacency structure exposing the same query surface so the capability is never lost. Render Mermaid text and SVG via FrankenMermaid for catalog graph --mermaid. This is an integral default capability, not optional.
acceptance_criteria: graph built from a fixture catalog; unit tests for ancestors/descendants/what-relates-to/reachability/cycle-detection; catalog graph --mermaid golden and SVG smoke test; fnx-* path-dep build OR documented in-house adjacency fallback exercised by the same test suite; cargo-tree admissibility proof; structured JSON-line logs.

title: Prepare open-source packaging and release readiness
description: Prepare public-facing packaging once MVP code exists: license/rider decision, crate metadata, docs.rs-ready examples, README quickstart, install guidance, CI proof lanes, no-forbidden-dependency checks, and release artifact checklist. Acceptance: release prep is blocked on working MVP but has explicit checklist and no premature claims.
acceptance_criteria: no-account test suite incl DPOR race suite and e2e harness green; single-asupersync-version gate and per-dependency admissibility proofs green; secret-leak compile gate and canary scans green; public-safety scan (text + beads DB/WAL strings) clean; LICENSE (MIT + OpenAI/Anthropic rider) and docs complete; CHANGELOG and versioning set; structured CI logs.

title: Finalize repo governance docs and crate boundary scaffold
description: Finalize repo governance docs and the crate-boundary skeleton (Phase 0, no connector logic). Crate skeletons: core, auth, http, sqlapi, catalog, frame, export, graph, tui, cache, cli, mcp, testkit (start the minimal five, scaffold the rest as boundaries clarify). Docs: AGENTS.md, README.md, the comprehensive plan, docs/asupersync_leverage.md, agent_cli_contract.md, security_model.md, dataset_manifest_contract.md, proof_lanes.md. Toolchain pin, [patch.crates-io] unification, deny.toml, and the single-asupersync-version CI gate are owned by the dedicated toolchain bead.
acceptance_criteria: workspace builds with empty crate skeletons; workspace lints active (forbid unsafe; deny unwrap/expect/panic/todo/dbg); all governance docs present and internally consistent; README/AGENTS reflect MCP + graph + tui + output modes; no secrets anywhere; public-safety scan clean.
close_reason: Phase 0 scaffold landed: Cargo workspace (resolver 3, edition 2024) with the minimal-five crate skeletons (core, auth, sqlapi, testkit, cli) building clean and producing the franken-snowflake binary; workspace lint policy active (forbid unsafe; deny clippy unwrap/expect/panic/todo/dbg) and proven to fail a build/clippy run; four governance docs added (agent_cli_contract.md, security_model.md, dataset_manifest_contract.md, proof_lanes.md) plus docs/protocol/ placeholder and .gitattributes golden-newline discipline; README/AGENTS already reflect MCP+graph+tui+output modes; secret + private-name public-safety scan clean. Toolchain pin / [patch.crates-io] / deny.toml / single-asupersync CI gate intentionally deferred to bead w0i.3; remaining 8 crates deferred to their boundary-owning beads per 'no blind sweep'.

title: Implement redaction, rights, and cost-safety guardrails
description: Implement redaction, rights, cost-safety, and provenance guardrails across CLI/MCP and library. No secret values in config/Debug/JSON/panic text. One composable redactor sourcing its needle list from a single shared constant (shared with the last-mile output scanner) with longest-prefix secret detection (eyJ/AKIA/ghp_/sk-/xoxb-/glpat-/AIza/...). Opaque cred_* handles in diagnostics; the env var name is never serialized. Fail-closed rights: an unknown label parses to the most restrictive class; an expired entitlement is treated as missing (never default-allow). COST MODEL (honest): the ENFORCEABLE guardrail is server-side STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_IN_SECONDS plus a result row cap on every query; the Budget cost quota and the unconstrained-query warning are ADVISORY telemetry layered on top, and a breach surfaces as Cancelled(CancelKind::CostBudget) -> distinct outcome_kind/exit code. Warehouse compute bills per second with a 60-SECOND MINIMUM per start/resume (model the floor, not pure per-second; auto-suspend/resume thrash re-incurs it). Cost vector on receipts (statements_run/partitions_fetched/bytes_scanned/warehouse_credits_estimate, the credit estimate labeled advisory). data_source provenance stamped on every envelope and a --require-live flag that refuses fixture-substituted data. The credential Debug-leak compile gate is a separate bead (w0i.5).
acceptance_criteria: redactor unit tests for every secret prefix and both header and query-param forms; fail-closed rights tests (unknown -> most restrictive; expired -> missing); a query lacking a server-side STATEMENT_TIMEOUT/row-cap is refused or auto-bounded; cost-vector accounting test with the credit estimate labeled advisory and the 60s-minimum reflected; CostBudget breach -> distinct outcome_kind/exit code; unconstrained-query warning test; --require-live refusal test; data_source stamping test on every envelope; canary-secret scan over all output channels (stdout/stderr/receipts/logs/exports); structured JSON-line logs.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan correction 2026-06-24 (Snowflake-docs-validated). Make the cost guardrail honest:

- Client-side warehouse-credit accounting is ADVISORY only -- credits cannot be metered precisely client-side. The ENFORCEABLE guardrail is server-side STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_IN_SECONDS plus a result row cap on every query. The Budget cost_quota and the unconstrained-query warning are advisory telemetry layered on top; a cost-quota breach surfaces as Cancelled(CancelKind::CostBudget) -> distinct outcome_kind/exit code.
- Warehouse compute bills per second with a 60-SECOND MINIMUM charged on every start/resume; auto-suspend/resume thrash re-incurs the minimum. The cost model must account for the 60s floor, not pure per-second.

Acceptance additions: a query without a server-side timeout/row-cap is refused or auto-bounded; cost estimate is labeled advisory in the receipt; 60s-minimum reflected in the warehouse_credits_estimate model.

Ref: docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/cost-understanding-compute (consulted 2026-06-24).

title: Capture Snowflake SQL API protocol schemas and goldens
description: Capture Snowflake SQL API request/response schemas as typed Rust with serde: SubmitStatementRequest/Response, QueryStatus, QueryFailureStatus, ResultSet, ResultSetMetaData (rowType with type/scale/precision/nullable/length), PartitionInfo (rowCount/compressedSize/uncompressedSize; top-level numRows = total across partitions), StatementCancelResponse, the parameters object, session parameters, query tags, and idempotency fields (requestId + retry=true). Bindings are POSITIONAL and TYPED ({"1":{type,value}}, value ALWAYS a string) by hard rule; never string interpolation. Model the HTTP status codes as DISTINCT states (do not conflate 429 with 202): 200 completed (ResultSet); 202 running/accepted-async (QueryStatus; the poll-again signal, also returned when a sync statement exceeds ~45s); 408 statement timeout (STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_IN_SECONDS); 422 failure (QueryFailureStatus); 429 server overload/rate-limit (Retry-After NOT guaranteed). jsonv2 result encoding: every data cell is a JSON STRING (the per-type decode math lives in the frame bead z1o) — the schema layer must type cells as strings and never infer from JSON shape. Multi-statement: MULTI_STATEMENT_COUNT (exact N, or 0=variable) -> statementHandles[]; bindings unsupported in that mode. The parameters object pins deterministic output (timezone, *_output_format, binary_output_format, use_cached_result, MULTI_STATEMENT_COUNT); the nullable query param controls NULL rendering (null vs "null") and is unrelated to rowType[].nullable. Ship official-example golden fixtures so the schemas are exercised before any live account exists. Refs: docs.snowflake.com SQL API submitting-requests / handling-responses / submitting-multiple-statements / reference (2026-06-24).
acceptance_criteria: serde round-trip golden tests for every schema type against official SQL API example JSON; status-code routing test proving 200/202/408/422/429 deserialize to distinct typed states; requestId + retry=true fields present and serialized; cells typed as strings (jsonv2) at the schema layer; null-handling and rowType scale/precision parsing tests; positional-binding serialization test (value-as-string, 1-based keys); multi-statement MULTI_STATEMENT_COUNT + statementHandles[] shape test and a bindings-refused-in-multi-statement test; parameters-object pinning test; PartitionInfo + numRows accounting test; deterministic key-sorted goldens with canonicalized volatile fields; structured JSON-line test logs per case.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Protocol anchors as of 2026-06-24: Snowflake SQL API overview/reference/authentication/response-handling docs. Capture exact doc URLs and fixture dates in tests. Do not use a third-party Rust Snowflake driver as authority or dependency.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan correction 2026-06-24 (Snowflake-docs-validated). Extend the protocol schemas to cover facts the first draft missed:

- Status codes are distinct, not interchangeable: 200=completed (ResultSet), 202=running/accepted-async (QueryStatus; the poll-again signal, also returned when a sync statement exceeds ~45s), 408=statement timeout (STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_IN_SECONDS), 422=failure (QueryFailureStatus), 429=server overload/rate limit (Retry-After NOT guaranteed). Model 408 explicitly; do not conflate 429 with 202.
- Idempotency contract: submit POST is not idempotent on its own; a client requestId (UUID) + retry=true query param makes resubmit safe (returns prior result instead of re-executing). Capture both fields in SubmitStatementRequest.
- jsonv2 wire encoding: every data cell is a JSON STRING decoded per its rowType, not by JSON shape. NUMBER/FIXED = plain decimal (do NOT divide by 10^scale); BOOLEAN = "true"/"false"; DATE = days-since-epoch; TIME/TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ = fractional epoch SECONDS; TIMESTAMP_TZ = "<epoch_sec.frac> <offset>" with offset = minutes+1440; BINARY = hex; VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY = embedded JSON. Docs are inconsistent on timestamp units, so the codec must be pinned by an empirical live golden.
- Multi-statement: gated by MULTI_STATEMENT_COUNT (exact N, or 0=variable); response returns statementHandles[]; bindings are NOT supported in multi-statement mode.
- parameters object pins deterministic output (timezone, *_output_format, binary_output_format, use_cached_result, MULTI_STATEMENT_COUNT). The nullable query param controls NULL rendering (null vs "null") and is unrelated to rowType[].nullable.

Refs: docs.snowflake.com SQL API submitting-requests, handling-responses, submitting-multiple-statements, reference (consulted 2026-06-24).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: [pane3/sqlapi domain] Prep draft landed at docs/protocol/schema_draft.md while this bead is blocked on w0i.15 (shared test harness). Implementation-ready spec: endpoints, the 200/202/408/422/429 status-code state machine, serde type sketch (camelCase + core newtypes), session params to pin, the jsonv2 wire-codec table, and a 14-fixture golden plan under crates/franken-snowflake-sqlapi/tests/fixtures/. Official Snowflake SQL API docs consulted 2026-06-24: developer-guide/sql-api/{index,reference,submitting-requests,handling-responses,authenticating}. jsonv2 timestamp unit (docs ambiguous: seconds vs nanos) stays pinned to the live golden in w0i.13. Claiming franken-snowflake-sqlapi + docs/protocol as my domain (agent-mail MCP unreachable this session; coordinating via git+beads). Will claim+implement the moment it goes READY, then fsnow-statement-lifecycle-ofl.

title: Implement statement submit, poll, partition fetch, and cancel lifecycle
description: Implement the statement lifecycle (submit -> poll -> partition fetch -> cancel) modeled as an Asupersync bracket so the remote cancel endpoint ALWAYS fires on drop/cancel and no Snowflake statement is orphaned. Concurrent partition fetch runs under one bounded Scope with child Budgets (meet()). Handle the distinct status codes: 200 completed; 202 still-running (poll the statement handle / statementStatusUrl; the not-yet-ready signal, also returned when a sync statement exceeds ~45s); 408 statement timeout (STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_IN_SECONDS) as a typed timeout outcome; 422 query failure; 429 rate-limiting (jittered exponential backoff; Retry-After is NOT guaranteed; distinct from 202, never a query-status code). Treat cancelled-during-connect as a DISTINCT receipt state (the asupersync TLS handshake is not cancel-safe; cancel mid-handshake drops the connection and no statement was submitted). Submit retry is OWNED here via custom jittered backoff keyed to a stable requestId + retry=true (the asupersync client RetryPolicy does NOT retry the non-idempotent POST; built-in retry only assists the idempotent GET poll/partition path; a safe resubmit returns the prior result rather than re-executing). gzip partitions (manual GzipDecompressor off content-encoding); positional typed bindings; multiple-statement refusal by default (when explicitly allowed: MULTI_STATEMENT_COUNT + statementHandles[] fan-out, bindings refused). Provide a RESULT_SCAN re-fetch path passing the stored Snowflake query_id (keyed by normalized-plan hash), subject to Snowflake result-cache retention (~24h); LAST_QUERY_ID only addresses the session-immediate statement.
acceptance_criteria: submit/poll/cancel happy-path test on the testkit; cancel triggers the remote cancel endpoint (verified via mock) under bracket; partition fetch concurrency bounded by Scope with child Budgets; 200/202/408/422/429 each handled as distinct states (202 poll-loop; 429 jittered backoff WITHOUT relying on Retry-After; 408 typed timeout outcome; 422 failure); cancelled-during-connect distinct-receipt-state test; idempotent-resubmit test (requestId + retry=true returns the prior result, no re-execute); gzip partition decode; multiple-statement refusal by default + a MULTI_STATEMENT_COUNT fan-out test when allowed (bindings refused); RESULT_SCAN by stored query_id re-fetch test; deep concurrency races covered by the DPOR suite; structured JSON-line logs with statement_handle and query_id (redacted profile).

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan correction 2026-06-24. Additions to the statement lifecycle (the 202/429 distinction and RESULT_SCAN-by-query_id are already captured -- good):

- Handle 408 (statement timeout, exceeded STATEMENT_TIMEOUT_IN_SECONDS) as a distinct typed timeout outcome, separate from a 422 query failure.
- Handle "cancelled during connect" as a distinct receipt state: the asupersync TLS handshake is not cancel-safe, so cancel mid-handshake means drop the connection -- not the same as a submitted-then-cancelled statement.
- Submit retry: the asupersync client RetryPolicy does NOT retry POST (non-idempotent). The lifecycle must own submit retries via custom jittered backoff, keyed to a stable requestId + retry=true so a resubmit returns the prior result instead of re-executing.
- 429: Retry-After is not guaranteed by Snowflake; use jittered exponential backoff and do not depend on the header.

Acceptance additions: 408 timeout outcome test; cancelled-during-connect receipt-state test; idempotent-resubmit (requestId+retry=true) test.

title: Plan optional Frankensearch text indexing for unstructured data
description: Design the optional text-indexing lane for Snowflake tables/views containing filings, transcripts, notes, JSON blobs, or other unstructured text. Frankensearch indexes selected text columns or exported chunks with provenance back to statement/query/dataset receipts, using ONLY the hash/lexical features (never fastembed/download, which pull ort/ONNX + openssl). Define a Reranker trait with a NO-OP default in the core search path; the pure-Rust frankensearch-rerank `native` cross-encoder (forbidden-dep-clean and cross-platform; runs on frankentorch tensors, no ort/ONNX) is a DEFERRED, feature-gated drop-in behind a `rerank` feature, added only if a long-text retrieval surface emerges. It is a reranker (one BERT forward per doc, ~45-120ms), so it is constrained to TOP-K (K<=50) refinement over long text columns -- never short-text catalog search or interactive ranking -- with caller-provided weights and a documented latency budget; never the fastembed-reranker/download path. Acceptance: design doc explains feature flags, rights propagation, indexing inputs, stable result handles, the Reranker-trait seam, and why each layer is optional.
acceptance_criteria: optional frankensearch indexing of unstructured text columns using ONLY the hash/lexical features (never fastembed/download); IndexBuilder add_document + TwoTierSearcher query test over fixture docs; a Reranker trait with a no-op default is defined and unit-tested (default leaves order unchanged); cargo-tree proof the offline indexing path pulls no tokio/reqwest/hyper/ort/onnx; if the optional rerank feature is wired, a cargo-tree proof it pulls no ort/onnx/openssl/tokio and a top-K-only enforcement test with a documented latency budget; feature-gated so the default build excludes both indexing and rerank; structured logs.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Update 2026-06-24: frankensearch now has a PURE-RUST semantic reranker that does NOT need ort/ONNX. Crate frankensearch-rerank v0.2.2, feature "native", type NativeReranker -- a cross-encoder (ms-marco-MiniLM-L6-v2) reimplemented on pure-Rust frankentorch tensors (frankensearch commit e717b8b, 2026-06-23, which DELETED the old ort-based path). cargo-tree of frankensearch-rerank --features native is forbidden-dep-clean (no tokio/ort/onnx/openssl/reqwest; pure-Rust tokenizer via fancy-regex; no compiled C/C++) and cross-platform (no CUDA/Metal; portable `wide` SIMD; runs Linux/macOS/Windows).

Caveat: it is a RERANKER, not a retriever -- one full BERT forward per (query,doc) pair (~45-120ms/doc), so it only fits TOP-K (K<=50) refinement over LONG unstructured text columns, not short-text catalog search (lexical/fuzzy wins there) and not interactive ranking. Weights (model_f32.safetensors + tokenizer.json, ~90MB) are caller-provided; do NOT enable the fastembed-reranker/download path (reintroduces ort+openssl).

Plan decision: keep hash/lexical as the core. Define a Reranker trait with a NO-OP default; wire frankensearch-rerank `native` behind a `rerank` feature as a deferred drop-in, added only if a long-text retrieval surface emerges. Acceptance if adopted: cargo-tree proof the rerank feature pulls no ort/onnx/openssl/tokio; top-K-only enforced; latency budget documented.
